g.text: Scene 1/5 — Inject rules at every prompt header
(UserPromptSubmit hook → ~490 token anchor)
$ echo {prompt} | karma_user_prompt_submit.py
[karma — long-term agreement anchor (compact form; full preferences injected at
session start)]
This turn: a quick name + one-line reminder of each direction. Full preference t
ext was injected at session start.
1. [long-term-fundamental] The user trusts you to dig into root causes. When fac
ing hard problems
2. [non-blocking-parallel] During sleep / wait / long-task execution, the user i
s waiting for your
3. [keep-pushing-no-stop] The user is fully-delegating — they expect you to auto
nomously continue
4. [read-before-write] Before editing code,
Scene 2/5 — Real-time block of sleep 30
(PreToolUse hook → non-blocking-parallel)
$ echo {tool=Bash, command=sleep 30} | karma_pre_tool_use.py
permissionDecision: deny
karma blocked: violation of 'non-blocking-parallel'.
Detected: Bash sleep command: 'sleep 30'
Direction: During sleep / wait / long-task execution, the user is waiting for
your
output. Staring at a progress bar isn't collaboration — it's "stuck."
Suggestion: sleep periods make the user wait; experience is 'stuck.' Switch to
run_in_background=True kicking off the task, then immediately push the next doa
ble thing (read related files / spawn another sub-agent / design next step) — ta
sk completion will notify you. For wait-on-condition, use Monitor + until loop.
Scene 3/5 — Catch short-term intent in Agent response
(Stop hook → long-term v0.11.0 response-level)
$ # Agent said: "Let me just hardcode this case to ship it"
$ karma_stop.py reads transcript, scans response
⚠ karma: Agent violated 'long-term-fundamental' — Your wording shows short-term
intent ('I'll patch this / hardcode temporarily / quick workaround') — user expe
cts long-term root cause
suggestion: 'Quick fix / workaround / temporary' intent comments = signal to
the user 'debt not paid.' Find the root-cause solution first; they'll feel more
at ease.
Scene 4/5 — Nudge Agent to keep pushing after silent stop
(Stop hook → keep-pushing-no-stop)
$ # Agent said: "Task complete." (pure statement, no next step)
⚠ karma: Agent violated 'keep-pushing-no-stop' — Response is pure-statement conc
lusion with no push signal / no decision question — real stop without a next ste
p
suggestion: The user is fully-delegating — after finishing a wave, they expec
t you to immediately find the next push point and continue, not silently stop an
d wait for feedback. If you need their judgment, ask clearly; if truly saturated
(reasonable stop), say where you're stuck so they know.
Scene 5/5 — Mid-conversation full reinject at decay threshold
(PostToolUse hook → byte_seq hits Opus 60K → full baseline)
$ # session state: tool_byte_seq=62000 (past Opus 60K threshold)
$ echo {tool=Read} | karma_post_tool_use.py
[karma — Your long-term agreement with the user]
You're collaborating with a real human user who listed several long-term priorit
ies.
This isn't rules and isn't a judgment — these are the collaborative agreements t
hey hope to build with you.
1. The user trusts you to dig into root causes. When facing hard problems
they want you to pause and think "what's the cleanest solution?"
rather than "what's the fastest patch?"
2. During sleep / wait...
That's karma — pure engineering, zero LLM.
github.com/jhaizhou-ops/karma
